verses.text: 'The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.' He makes His sheep lie in green pastures, leads them by still waters, and restores the soul. The Shepherd supplies, guides, and revives. To belong to Him is to lack no good thing the soul truly needs.

'The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.' He makes His sheep lie in green pastures, leads them by still waters, and restores the soul. The Shepherd supplies, guides, and revives. To belong to Him is to lack no good thing the soul truly needs.

'The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.' He makes His sheep lie in green pastures, leads them by still waters, and restores the soul. The Shepherd supplies, guides, and revives. To belong to Him is to lack no good thing the soul truly needs.

Even through 'the valley of the shadow of death' the sheep fears no evil, for the Shepherd is there, His rod and staff a comfort. His presence turns terror into peace. The darkest path is safe when walked beside Him.

He spreads a table before His own in the presence of foes, anoints the head, and fills the cup to overflowing, so that goodness and mercy follow all their days, and they dwell in His house for ever. Such is the believer's portion. Provided now and welcomed home at last, the sheep of the Lord want nothing.
